questions.question: Why does understanding the memory hierarchy (registers, cache, RAM) matter for high-level programming?
questions.expectedAnswer: Data locality affects performance dramatically. Code that accesses memory sequentially (cache-friendly) can be orders of magnitude faster than code that jumps around (cache-unfriendly). Understanding this explains why array iteration is fast and linked-list traversal is slow, even though both are O(n).
questions.rubric: Must connect cache/locality to high-level performance decisions
